llm_values.evidence: In step 22, the agent performs its first configuration change (toggling mail.quoted_graphical to false). For the remainder of the trajectory (steps 23-50), it continues to guess other preferences and eventually enters a terminal loop (grepping for ">" in the mail profile) without ever once checking if the quoting behavior in the Thunderbird GUI had actually changed. This failure to evaluate task progress is the root cause of the eventual timeout.
llm_values.correction: At step 22, after toggling the 'mail.quoted_graphical' preference, the agent should have attempted to reply to an existing email to verify if the indentation and ">" character were removed. By testing its assumptions, the agent would have discovered that its changes were ineffective (or even counter-productive) and could have adjusted its plan instead of continuing to make unverified changes and eventually wasting steps in the terminal.

human_values.evidence: In the first 20 steps, the agent has been looking for the correct interface to change the format of the quoted message.
human_values.correction: The agent should: go to Account Settings → Composition & Addressing for the account, then uncheck “Automatically quote the original message when replying”
